column3:
View and add buttons clear to see; a shortcut to the view page by clicking the title of the trial would have been intuitive too. 
Q3 was fairly straightforward once I spotted what information i was looking for. I expected it to be directly in a subtitle title of the trials (because of the importance it seemed to have on the task 3 and the following) .
column7: A few bugs seemed to have occurred: the download couldn't be done, and the submission button did not provide any feedback nor actualised the data.
column9: I had to ask for help for: finding the sector information in the trial view, and to handle the bugs described above.
column11: Provided the bugs did not occur, I think I would have been able to complete the tasks efficiently.
column13: It required a bit of adaptation at first, when I had not read through it all and hence did not know anything about its content.
column15: Past the first few seconds (task 3), which allowed me to become more familiar with the nature of the content and the website, it became quite intuitive.
column17: If I have a database that I need to search through and update regularly, I believe this (working) interface would make me productive quickly.
column19:
Submission button did not provide any feedback (and submission seemed to indeed have failed).
There was also a download problem, but it still is not clear what happened.
There could have been a line reading "no result" when sorting the data led to no match.
column21: Help was occasionally needed, but I believe it remained minimal, and I was then able to quickly and easily continue.
column23:

column25: I struggle with finding the "sector" since I was browsing through subtitles (and, at that time, I wasn't sure what the "sector" implied here was).

column37:
View, sort, and update the dataset.
Maybe a summary page that synthesises the data (in a collection, for example) would be good too, but it isn't a must.
column39: Few bugs occurred (download and submission), and I couldn't find immediately the "sector" button on the view page. But all good otherwise.

column3: Other than some presentation elements (indicating the filtering criteria using a filter symbol, uneven tile view, etc), I was satisfied with how easy it is to use the system.
column7: I think I would find myself downloading too many collections, and I don't exactly know how to filter for multiple criteria at a time.
column13: The general UI is a little hard to get used to immediately. Light margins between the filter and the collection tiles, and moving from a separate tab for creating a collection tile to an entirely different icon (like a plus on the bottom left of the screen) would make it more intuitive to access.
column17: It can be useful for poring over and sorting data but it needs more criteria
column21: I don't think I made a mistake
column25: I wasn't sure what information to look for.
column27: Some of the view names were inconsistent between the instructions and the system
column31: Personal preference: for textual information like domain and country, I prefer to have an autocompletable search bar to find exactly the information I'm looking for.
column37: It would be cool if I could add a collection by DOI or other external identifiers.

column3: Buttons and navigation were easy to spot. Clicking a trial title to jump straight into its view would feel natural, and key metadata like sector could stand out more in the trial header.
column7: Sorting and filtering were generally clear once I realised where the controls were. Multi-select filters (or visible filter chips) would help when combining criteria, and a clear reset would avoid confusion.
column13: The layout took a minute to learn (especially the relationship between filters and tiles), but once learned it was consistent. Small affordances like tooltips on filter icons would reduce the initial friction.
column17: Useful for scanning and updating a dataset, and I would use it again if I could save common filters or views.
column19: One download attempt stalled without an error, and the submit action could use a confirmation message so it is clear the update succeeded.
column21: I accidentally created an empty collection because the create action was enabled before selecting anything; some validation would help.
column23: A short in-product hint (or one-page help) would be enough; I did not need extensive documentation.
column25: Sector meaning was not obvious at first; a brief description or example would help users interpret it correctly.
column27: Minor inconsistency in naming between the task instructions and the UI labels, but it did not block me.
column31: Autocomplete search for text fields (country, domain, etc.) would speed up precise filtering compared with scrolling long lists.
column37: Being able to create a collection by DOI / registry ID would be a strong quality-of-life improvement.

column3: Overall the workflow (view, sort, update) felt straightforward. I would still expect important fields like sector to be visually emphasised and searchable from the top of the trial view.
column7: Filtering was quick for single criteria, but it was not obvious how to apply multiple filters at once; showing active filter chips would make this clearer.
column13: The interface looked a little dense on first contact, but after one task it became predictable. More spacing between tiles and clearer grouping of filter controls would improve scanability.
column17: It would be productive for regular database maintenance, especially if collections could be named and revisited easily.
column19: No errors encountered in this run.
column21: I do not think I made any mistakes; the system behaved as expected.
column23: Documentation was limited, but the UI was mostly self-explanatory; a quick start guide would still help first-time users.
column27: Some labels differed slightly from the wording in the task sheet, which slowed me down briefly.
column31: I would prefer an autocomplete field for country/domain and a "no results" state when filters return nothing.
